blast_radius.summary: Low-Medium. Five tasks touching 2 modified files and 6 created files, all within the catalog component subtree. No composition roots, no shared API contracts, no database files. The weighted score is low; the primary risk is cross-plan vocabulary fidelity, not blast width.

files.reason: Extend PluginSummary interface; add InlineChatSourceEntry, DataTrustTier
Major rewrite: new layout, remove Use-in-pipeline machinery
Centralized flag-to-metadata table; single source of truth for rendering

blast_radius.weighted_score: 7.0 (PluginCard 2x; auditCharacteristics 1x; types 1x; 6x 0.5x for tests and leaf components)
blast_radius.recommendation: Proceed after resolving the blocking issues. No case for phasing; the scope is already tightly bounded. CatalogDrawer.tsx is NOT modified in 16b, which is the root of one of the blockers — see B4.

one_way_doors.change: Deletion of 'Use in pipeline' button, buildInsertionPrompt, handleUseInPipeline, and onCloseDrawer prop from PluginCard.tsx
one_way_doors.mitigation: Sufficient. Pre-RC5 has no documented users; per CLAUDE.md No-Legacy Policy, deferring breaking changes is the opposite of what is wanted. The replacement workflow (InlineChatSourceEntry prefill path in 16c) is specified. PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT export is explicitly retained so ChatInput.tsx keeps its listener. However, see B4 — the onCloseDrawer prop deletion leaves a TypeScript compile error in CatalogDrawer.tsx that is NOT addressed within 16b.

one_way_doors.change: PluginCard.tsx disclosure affordance changes: header div role=button replaced by an explicit <button> element with aria-label='Schema for {plugin.name}'
one_way_doors.mitigation: Adequate. This resolves a documented WAI-ARIA nested-interactive violation (the old structural-invariant test in PluginCard.test.tsx pinned this structure). The new schema-only button is labelled correctly. The existing collapsed-header tests cover the new label pattern.

blocking_issues.title: auditCharacteristics.ts uses 'emits_provenance' flag but 16a VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS and wire format use 'provenance'
blocking_issues.description: 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS constant (16a-phase-7a-backend.md:1190) includes 'provenance' as the valid author-declared flag string. The 16a round-2 review note (2026-05-17) explicitly corrects 'emits_provenance' -> 'provenance' in the backend test fixtures. However, 16b's auditCharacteristics.ts implementation (16b plan:563) still defines flag: 'emits_provenance', and the auditCharacteristics.test.ts (16b plan:407) still asserts lookupAuditCharacteristic('emits_provenance') != null. The consequence is that any plugin declaring audit_characteristics={'provenance'} (the valid backend string) will lookup-miss in 16b's table and fall through to the 'unknown' chip fallback, rendering the raw string 'provenance' as label rather than the intended 'extra provenance' display with its glyph and tooltip. The auditCharacteristics.test.ts completeness test title even names 'provenance' in the it() description ('exposes provenance / retention / quarantine / coerce / signed') but asserts the wrong flag string.
evidence: 16a-phase-7a-backend.md:2323 (round-2 note) — 'Corrected test fixture "emits_provenance" → "provenance" to match 08-catalog-reshape.md authoritative vocabulary'
blocking_issues.required_resolution: In 16b Task 2, change flag: 'emits_provenance' to flag: 'provenance' in the AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS array. Update the test on line 407 from lookupAuditCharacteristic('emits_provenance') to lookupAuditCharacteristic('provenance'). The label, glyph, and tooltip content stay the same; only the lookup key changes.

blocking_issues.title: auditCharacteristics.ts has no entry for 'retention' flag despite it being a valid member of VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS
blocking_issues.description: 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS constant (16a-phase-7a-backend.md:1191) includes 'retention' as a valid author-declared flag string — one of the 7 non-determinism-derived members. 16b's AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS array does not include a 'retention' entry. The test (16b plan:406) names 'retention' in its it() description ('exposes provenance / retention / quarantine / coerce / signed') but does not actually assert lookupAuditCharacteristic('retention') — the assertion line for retention is absent from the test body. Any plugin declaring audit_characteristics={'retention'} will fall through to the 'unknown' fallback chip. The forward-compatibility 'unknown' path is explicitly designed for flags added in the FUTURE without a frontend metadata update; 'retention' is a current, defined, in-vocabulary flag that should have a metadata entry.
evidence: 16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:406 — test it() title names retention but the test body (lines 407-411) does not assert it
16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:482-592 — AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS array has no entry with flag: 'retention'
blocking_issues.required_resolution: Add a 'retention' entry to AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS in auditCharacteristics.ts with appropriate label, glyph, tooltip, and tone. Add the missing assertion lookupAuditCharacteristic('retention') to the test body on line 406.

blocking_issues.title: auditCharacteristics.ts has no entry for 'network' flag; 16a VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS distinguishes 'network' (author-declared) from 'external_call' (determinism-derived)
blocking_issues.description: 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS constant (16a-phase-7a-backend.md:1195) includes 'network' as a valid author-declared flag. The 16b 2026-05-15 reality-review note collapsed 'network' -> 'external_call' in the frontend lookup key but left 'network' in VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS on the backend. These are two distinct flags: 'external_call' is derived from Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL and always present on external-call plugins; 'network' is an author-declared characteristic for plugins that make network calls without being classified EXTERNAL_CALL (e.g., a transform that does auxiliary health-check pings). Design doc 08-catalog-reshape.md (cited in 16a at line 391-400) describes capability_tags=['http', 'network', 'scraping'] for a web-scrape transform, implying 'network' as a distinct declared characteristic. Any plugin declaring audit_characteristics={'network'} produces an 'unknown' chip with the raw string 'network' as label, rather than the intended metadata-driven rendering.
evidence: 16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:1359 — review note: 'Changed auditCharacteristics.ts lookup key and all test flag strings from "network" to "external_call"' — this removed 'network' as a first-class key
16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:482-592 — AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS array has no entry with flag: 'network'
blocking_issues.required_resolution: Add a 'network' entry to AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS in auditCharacteristics.ts alongside the existing 'external_call' entry. 'network' is the author-declared flag for plugins that make network calls not captured by the determinism-derived 'external_call' path. Add a corresponding test assertion. The two flags have different tones and tooltips: 'external_call' is derived (attention, 'Reaches an external system over the network'); 'network' is declared (attention, 'Makes network requests that are captured by the determinism-derivation if EXTERNAL_CALL, or declared explicitly for auxiliary network use').

blocking_issues.title: CatalogDrawer.tsx passes onCloseDrawer to PluginCard but 16b deletes that prop without modifying CatalogDrawer.tsx — TypeScript build fails
blocking_issues.description: The current CatalogDrawer.tsx (line 359) passes onCloseDrawer={onClose} to PluginCard. 16b Task 5 deletes onCloseDrawer from PluginCardProps (the prop no longer exists on the rewritten component). However, 16b's file structure section (plan:149) marks CatalogDrawer.tsx as 'Not modified.' After Task 5 lands, npx tsc --noEmit will fail at the CatalogDrawer.tsx call site with 'Property onCloseDrawer does not exist on type PluginCardProps.' The plan's Task 1 Step 5 runs tsc before Task 5 (the rewrite), so this will not be caught early; the Task 5 verification only runs npx vitest (not tsc). The plan's description of the intermediate state ('if 7B ships alone, the user sees a new card layout — that state is acceptable') presupposes the build compiles, but it will not.
evidence: 16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:1078-1090 — new PluginCardProps interface in Task 5 has no onCloseDrawer field
16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:1265 — Task 5 Step 5 runs 'npx vitest run src/components/catalog/ src/components/chat/' — no tsc
blocking_issues.required_resolution: Choose one of: (a) Keep onCloseDrawer?: () => void as an optional no-op prop in the rewritten PluginCardProps (16b Task 5) — the prop is retained but unused until 16c's InlineChatSourceEntry uses the drawer-close path directly. This is the lowest-change resolution. (b) Add CatalogDrawer.tsx to 16b's modified-files list and explicitly remove the onCloseDrawer={onClose} call-site in Task 5 Step 3. Then 16c's InlineChatSourceEntry handles drawer close independently via its own onCloseDrawer prop. Option (a) is lower blast radius and matches how the plan was described (16c fixes up the drawer); option (b) is cleaner but expands 16b scope. Add a tsc gate to Task 5 Step 5 regardless of which option is chosen.

warnings.title: title attribute as sole tooltip surface is not screen-reader-reliable
warnings.description: AuditCharacteristicIcon and TrustTierBadge both use the HTML title attribute as the tooltip delivery mechanism. The plan explicitly notes this is 'without pulling in a tooltip library' (plan:658). However, title attribute tooltips are not reliably announced by screen readers in modern configurations: NVDA and VoiceOver both require specific user settings to read title attributes, and touch devices provide no mechanism for hovering. The tooltip content (especially the tier explanations) is substantive — 'Tier 3: External data crossing into the pipeline. Zero trust.' is the primary explanation of what the tier badge means. Hiding it behind a title-hover is a meaningful accessibility gap, not a cosmetic issue. The test at plan:655-660 asserts the title attribute is present (testing the wrong thing — that the attribute exists, not that users can access it).
warnings.required_resolution: This is flagged as MAJOR not BLOCKER because the MVP function is not blocked — icons still render and labels are visible. However, before the staging smoke, a brief tooltip strategy should be established: either accept a lightweight Radix UI Tooltip (already used elsewhere in the codebase if present, check imports) or add aria-describedby with an off-screen text element. The 'without a tooltip library' constraint in the plan should be verified against existing codebase tooltip usage before accepting it as an architectural constraint.

warnings.title: initialExpanded test-only prop leaks into production PluginCardProps interface
warnings.description: The plan introduces initialExpanded?: boolean on PluginCardProps (plan:1086) with a comment 'Test-only: start in the expanded state to assert schema rendering.' This is a legitimate test ergonomics choice when the alternative (click the button in the test) requires async waitFor for a side-effect-free assertion. However, leaking test-only props into production interfaces is an anti-pattern that accumulates over time. A cleaner alternative is to test the expanded state by using userEvent.click on the Schema button and awaiting the expanded content, as the plan itself acknowledges (plan:1006-1010). If the async path is chosen in the implementation, delete initialExpanded. If retained, document it explicitly as test-only at the interface level.
warnings.required_resolution: Either (a) remove initialExpanded and test schema rendering via userEvent.click + await; or (b) retain it with a JSDoc '@internal For testing only. Do not use in production render trees.' The plan already hedges ('If you'd prefer to keep the toggle entirely internal, replace that test') — the implementer should resolve this before committing.

warnings.title: KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS typed as string[] rather than readonly string[]
warnings.description: The plan's auditCharacteristics.ts exports KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS: string[] (plan:596). Because this is a derived array from the AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS const (which is also mutable), callers could push() to it. The correct type for an export that should be treated as a fixed list is readonly string[] or a tuple. This is consistent with how the codebase uses 'as const' for similar fixed-list exports (e.g., VALIDATION_CHECK_OUTCOME_CODE_VALUES in types/index.ts uses 'as const' with a const tuple).
warnings.required_resolution: Change KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS: string[] to KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS: readonly string[] and derive it as AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS.map((m) => m.flag) as const or Array.from the map. No functional change; improves type safety at call sites.

warnings.title: No tsc gate added to Task 5 verification step
warnings.description: Task 5 Step 5 ('Run the rest of the catalog and chat suites for regressions') runs only vitest, not tsc. Task 1 Step 5 runs tsc, but that runs before the PluginCard rewrite; any type errors introduced by the rewrite (including B4) are not caught. A final tsc --noEmit after the PluginCard rewrite is the mechanically correct gate for 'this commit does not break the build.'
warnings.required_resolution: Add 'npx tsc --noEmit' to Task 5 Step 5 after the vitest run, before the commit. This is one extra line and catches prop-interface drift between PluginCard and its call sites.

recommendations.title: Component decomposition is sound — leaf + centralized-table pattern is the right architecture
recommendations.description: The AuditCharacteristicIcon + TrustTierBadge + centralized auditCharacteristics.ts decomposition is correct. Centralizing the flag-to-metadata mapping prevents the drift risk (where the card and the filter chip strip disagree on what a flag means). The unknown-flag fallback is the right forward-compatibility mechanism — a backend flag addition that outpaces the frontend update renders gracefully rather than crashing. The ProseSection helper in the rewritten PluginCard is the right micro-extraction (single-responsibility, no prop threading). No architectural changes recommended here.

recommendations.title: Scope-split between 16b and 16c is honest but B4 makes it incomplete — resolve B4 to restore the boundary
recommendations.description: The primitives-vs-integration split is architecturally sound: 16b ships leaf components and card rewrite; 16c wires them into the drawer (FilterChipStrip, InlineChatSourceEntry, CatalogDrawer integration). The split fails only because 16b deletes the onCloseDrawer prop without owning the CatalogDrawer.tsx call-site cleanup (B4). Resolving B4 (either retain the optional prop or explicitly modify CatalogDrawer.tsx) restores the honesty of the boundary. Once B4 is resolved, the plan's claim that 16b can ship alone and produce a visible but integration-incomplete state is accurate.

recommendations.title: PluginSummary type shape matches 16a wire format; the divergence is value-vocabulary only
recommendations.description: The TypeScript PluginSummary extension (plan:274-287) correctly mirrors the 16a Pydantic wire shape: string | null for prose fields, string[] for arrays (not tuple[str, ...] which is a Python-ism), DataTrustTier = 1 | 2 | 3 literal union matching the Pydantic ge=1, le=3 constraint. No schema shape mismatch. The blockers are flag-string vocabulary mismatches inside the auditCharacteristics.ts value table, not shape mismatches in the TypeScript interface.

recommendations.title: Retained PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT export and schema-disclosure code path preservation are correct decisions
recommendations.description: The plan correctly retains the PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT constant export at PluginCard.tsx:17 to preserve the ChatInput.tsx import path. The schema-disclosure code (isDiscriminated, resolveVariants, renderFields) is reproduced verbatim from the current PluginCard.tsx — this preserves the existing schema-rendering behaviors pinned by the current test suite (flat schema, discriminated union, dangling $ref, missing propertyName fallback). These tests are currently passing; the rewrite keeps them passing by keeping the code path identical.

recommendations.title: CatalogDrawer.test.tsx mock fixtures need updating in Task 1 Step 5 — plan correctly identifies this
recommendations.description: Task 1 Step 5 correctly identifies CatalogDrawer.test.tsx and PluginCard.test.tsx as the two fixture files that will fail tsc when PluginSummary gains required new fields. The instruction to add null/[] defaults to existing fixture constructions is the correct resolution; narrowing the type to make existing fixtures compile is explicitly disallowed. This is well-handled in the plan.

pattern_alignment.component_architecture: ALIGNED — AuditCharacteristicIcon and TrustTierBadge follow the codebase's established leaf-component pattern (single responsibility, pure presentation, no internal state, CSS-class-driven styling). PluginCard rewrite maintains the controlled/uncontrolled disclosure toggle pattern from the current implementation.
pattern_alignment.tdd_shape: ALIGNED — All five tasks follow the failing-test-first pattern with explicit run commands and expected outcomes. The Task 2 test is written as a completeness guard (iterating over expected flags), which is the right pattern for a closed-vocabulary table.
pattern_alignment.typescript_type_discipline: ALIGNED — DataTrustTier = 1 | 2 | 3 literal union forces discriminator reads at render time; TIER_TOOLTIPS: Record<1 | 2 | 3, string> exhaustively maps all tiers; the TrustTierBadge null branch is explicit. Consistent with how the codebase handles finite-value taxonomies (see RUN_STATUS_VALUES, VALIDATION_CHECK_OUTCOME_CODE_VALUES in types/index.ts).
pattern_alignment.a11y_pattern: PARTIAL — The new disclosure button (Schema for {plugin.name}) and aria-expanded are improvements over the current div-with-role-button pattern. The title-tooltip surface is the main gap (see W1).
pattern_alignment.forward_compatibility: ALIGNED — The unknown-flag fallback in AuditCharacteristicIcon is the correct mechanism. The lookupAuditCharacteristic return type is AuditCharacteristicMeta | null (not undefined), forcing callers to handle the null case explicitly.
pattern_alignment.test_fixture_hygiene: PARTIAL — The plan correctly identifies the fixture files that need updating (CatalogDrawer.test.tsx, PluginCard.test.tsx) and gives specific instructions. The test for 'exposes provenance / retention / quarantine / coerce / signed' (plan:406) names retention and provenance in the description but the body has gaps (see B1, B2) — a misleading test title that would pass even with missing coverage.

cross_plan_integrity.16a_type_shape_to_16b: SOUND — PluginSummary TypeScript extension (plan:274-287) matches 16a Pydantic wire shape exactly. string[] for arrays, DataTrustTier = 1 | 2 | 3 matches ge=1, le=3, all prose fields string | null.
cross_plan_integrity.16a_vocabulary_to_16b: BROKEN (x3) — Three flag-string vocabulary mismatches: (1) 'emits_provenance' in 16b vs 'provenance' in 16a VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS; (2) 'retention' present in 16a vocabulary, absent from 16b table; (3) 'network' present in 16a vocabulary, absent from 16b table after 2026-05-15 review collapsed it into 'external_call'. See B1, B2, B3.
cross_plan_integrity.16b_prop_delete_to_16c: BROKEN — 16b deletes onCloseDrawer from PluginCardProps but CatalogDrawer.tsx (which 16c modifies) still passes it; neither 16b nor 16c owns the call-site cleanup explicitly. See B4.
cross_plan_integrity.16b_PREFILL_export_to_16c: SOUND — PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT export is explicitly retained at PluginCard.tsx:17. 16c's InlineChatSourceEntry.tsx imports it from './PluginCard' (16c plan:505), which will resolve correctly after 16b Task 5.
cross_plan_integrity.16b_InlineChatSourceEntry_type_to_16c: SOUND — InlineChatSourceEntry type (plan:304-311) is defined in 16b types/index.ts with the shape 16c's InlineChatSourceEntry.tsx component expects (kind, name, description, example_prompt). The component itself is in 16c; the type-only addition in 16b is a clean forward declaration.

summary: Four blocking issues must be resolved before implementation begins. B1-B3 are flag-vocabulary mismatches between 16a's finalized VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS (13-member closed set) and 16b's auditCharacteristics.ts metadata table: (B1) the plan uses 'emits_provenance' as a flag key but 16a's authoritative vocabulary uses 'provenance' — corrected in 16a's round-2 review but not propagated to 16b; (B2) 'retention' appears in 16a VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS but has no metadata entry and no test assertion in 16b; (B3) 'network' is a valid author-declared flag distinct from the determinism-derived 'external_call', but 16b's 2026-05-15 review collapsed the two, leaving no 'network' metadata entry. All three produce 'unknown' fallback chips for flags that have defined backend vocabulary, silently degrading display quality. B4 is a TypeScript compile break: 16b deletes the onCloseDrawer prop from PluginCard but marks CatalogDrawer.tsx as 'Not modified', leaving a call-site type error that prevents build. The architecture itself — leaf component decomposition, centralized metadata table, unknown-flag fallback, PluginSummary type extension — is sound. The blockers are vocabulary fidelity and a scope-boundary gap, not structural design flaws. Four warnings round out the review: a title-attribute accessibility gap (W1, MAJOR), a test-only prop bleeding into production types (W2, MINOR), KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS mutability (W3, NIT), and a missing tsc gate in Task 5 (W4, MINOR).
